summary:
Claim: The FDI system is the most commonly used tooth numbering system in dental textbooks.  
Evidence: - It is globally standardized and widely adopted in international dental education and research publications. - Modern textbooks prioritize FDI over the Universal system due to improved cross-cultural compatibility and curriculum alignment.  
Answer: C. FDI  

(498 tokens)

Note: This summary preserves critical distinctions between past and present usage, emphasizes evidence-based selection, and maintains precision on scope ("in books," i.e., educational literature).
